name: Taittiriya Upanishad
summary: The Taittiriya Upanishad has three sections: Shiksha Valli (on phonetics and ethics), Brahmananda Valli (on the five sheaths and Brahman as bliss), and Bhrigu Valli (Bhrigu's progressive discovery of Brahman). It famously declares: satyam jnanam anantam brahma — Brahman is truth, knowledge, infinity.

verses.sanskrit:
सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं ब्रह्म | यो वेद निहितं गुहायां परमे व्योमन् |
सोऽश्नुते सर्वान् कामान् सह | ब्रह्मणा विपश्चितेति ||२.१.१||
verses.transliteration:
satyaṃ jñānam anantaṃ brahma | yo veda nihitaṃ guhāyāṃ parame vyoman |
so'śnute sarvān kāmān saha | brahmaṇā vipaściteti ||2.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: hidden in the cave (of the heart)

verses.english_translation: Brahman is truth, knowledge, infinity. One who knows it hidden in the cave of the heart, in the highest ether, attains all desires together with the all-knowing Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सत्यम् — परिणामरहितम्। ज्ञानम् — चैतन्यस्वरूपम्। अनन्तम् — देशकालवस्तुपरिच्छेदरहितम्। एतत् ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Satyam — that which does not undergo transformation. Jnanam — whose very nature is consciousness. Anantam — unlimited by space, time, or object. This is Brahman. One who knows this hidden in the heart attains all.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नमयो ह्येवायं पुरुषः | तस्येदमेव शिरः | अयं दक्षिणः पक्षः | अयमुत्तरः पक्षः | अयमात्मा | इदं पुच्छं प्रतिष्ठा ||२.१.२||
verses.transliteration: annamayo hy evāyaṃ puruṣaḥ | tasyedam eva śiraḥ | ayaṃ dakṣiṇaḥ pakṣaḥ | ayam uttaraḥ pakṣaḥ | ayam ātmā | idaṃ pucchaṃ pratiṣṭhā ||2.1.2||
verses.english_translation: This person is indeed made of the essence of food. This is his head, this his right wing, this his left wing, this his trunk, this his tail — the foundation.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नमयः — अन्नरसपरिणामः स्थूलशरीरम्। पञ्चकोशानां मध्ये बाह्यतमः कोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The annamaya kosha (food sheath) is the gross body, a transformation of the essence of food. It is the outermost of the five sheaths. The teaching progressively moves inward to discover the true Self beyond all sheaths.

verses.sanskrit:
आनन्दो ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | आनन्दाद्ध्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते |
आनन्देन जातानि जीवन्ति | आनन्दं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.६.१||
verses.transliteration:
ānando brahmeti vyajānāt | ānandād dhy eva khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante |
ānandena jātāni jīvanti | ānandaṃ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.6.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: Bliss is Brahman. For from bliss alone are all these beings born; by bliss they live once born; into bliss they return at death.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आनन्दः — परमानन्दः — ब्रह्मैव। सर्वाणि भूतानि आनन्दात् जायन्ते — ब्रह्मण एव उत्पद्यन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bliss is Brahman itself. All beings are born from Brahman-bliss, sustained by it, and return to it. This is Bhrigu's final realization after progressive inquiry — the ultimate ground is pure bliss (ananda).

verses.sanskrit: ॐ शं नो मित्रः शं वरुणः | शं नो भवत्वर्यमा | शं न इन्द्रो बृहस्पतिः | शं नो विष्णुरुरुक्रमः | नमो ब्रह्मणे | नमस्ते वायो | त्वमेव प्रत्यक्षं ब्रह्मासि | त्वामेव प्रत्यक्षं ब्रह्म वदिष्यामि | ऋतं वदिष्यामि | सत्यं वदिष्यामि | तन्मामवतु | तद्वक्तारमवतु | अवतु माम् | अवतु वक्तारम् ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: oṃ śaṃ no mitraḥ śaṃ varuṇaḥ | śaṃ no bhavatv aryamā | śaṃ na indro bṛhaspatiḥ | śaṃ no viṣṇur urukramaḥ | namo brahmaṇe | namas te vāyo | tvam eva pratyakṣaṃ brahmāsi | tvām eva pratyakṣaṃ brahma vadiṣyāmi | ṛtaṃ vadiṣyāmi | satyaṃ vadiṣyāmi | tan mām avatu | tad vaktāram avatu | avatu mām | avatu vaktāram ||1.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: you are the directly perceived Brahman
I shall speak the truth (cosmic order)

verses.english_translation: Om. May Mitra be auspicious to us; may Varuna be auspicious; may Aryaman be auspicious; may Indra and Brihaspati be auspicious; may Vishnu of wide strides be auspicious. Salutation to Brahman. Salutation to you, O Vayu — you are indeed the directly perceived Brahman. I shall declare you as the directly perceived Brahman. I shall speak the cosmic truth. I shall speak personal truth. May that protect me. May that protect the teacher. May it protect me. May it protect the teacher.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: शं नः मित्रः — मित्रादयः देवाः अस्माकम् शुभकराः भवन्तु। त्वम् प्रत्यक्षम् ब्रह्म — वायुः प्रत्यक्षः — ब्रह्मणः प्रतिनिधिः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This opening prayer invokes all the cosmic deities for auspiciousness. Vayu (wind/prana) is called the 'directly perceived Brahman' because among all cosmic forces, prana is most immediately experienced. The student vows to speak both cosmic truth (rita) and personal truth (satya).

verses.sanskrit: शीक्षां व्याख्यास्यामः | वर्णः स्वरः | मात्रा बलम् | साम सन्तानः | इत्युक्तः शीक्षाध्यायः ||१.२.१||
verses.transliteration: śīkṣāṃ vyākhyāsyāmaḥ | varṇaḥ svaraḥ | mātrā balam | sāma santānaḥ | ity uktaḥ śīkṣādhyāyaḥ ||1.2.1||
verses.english_translation: We shall explain phonetics: sound, accent, quantity, force, evenness, and continuity. Thus is stated the chapter on phonetics.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: शीक्षा — वेदाङ्गम् — वर्णोच्चारणशास्त्रम्। वर्णः — अक्षरम्। स्वरः — उदात्तादिः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Shiksha (phonetics) is the first of the six Vedic auxiliaries. It teaches the correct pronunciation of Vedic sounds. The six elements — sound, accent, quantity, force, evenness, and continuity — ensure the Vedic mantras are chanted correctly for their full effect.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यं वद | धर्मं चर | स्वाध्यायान्मा प्रमदः | आचार्याय प्रियं धनमाहृत्य प्रजातन्तुं मा व्यवच्छेत्सीः ||१.११.१||
verses.transliteration: satyaṃ vada | dharmaṃ cara | svādhyāyān mā pramadaḥ | ācāryāya priyaṃ dhanam āhṛtya prajātantuṃ mā vyavacchetsīḥ ||1.11.1||

word_meanings.meaning: bring pleasing wealth to the teacher

verses.english_translation: Speak truth. Practice dharma. Do not neglect self-study. Having brought pleasing wealth to the teacher, do not cut the thread of progeny.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सत्यम् वद — सत्यम् एव वद — असत्यम् मा वद। धर्मम् चर — धर्माचरणम् कुरु। स्वाध्यायात् मा प्रमदः — वेदाध्ययनम् मा त्यज।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This is the teacher's farewell instruction to the graduating student. Speak truth, practice dharma, continue Vedic study, honor the teacher with gifts, and continue the tradition. These are the foundational ethical instructions of the Taittiriya Upanishad.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्मादन्नरसमयात् | अन्योऽन्तर आत्मा प्राणमयः | तेनैष पूर्णः ||२.२.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmād annarasamayāt | anyo'ntara ātmā prāṇamayaḥ | tenaiṣa pūrṇaḥ ||2.2.1||
verses.english_translation: Different from this one made of food-essence is another inner Self made of prana. By that, this one is filled.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नरसमयात् — स्थूलशरीरात् — अन्यः — भिन्नः — प्राणमयः आत्मा — प्राणकोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Within the gross body (annamaya kosha) is a subtler body made of prana (pranamaya kosha). The prana-body fills and animates the food-body. This is the second of the five sheaths, progressively subtler as we move inward toward the true Self.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्मात्प्राणमयात् | अन्योऽन्तर आत्मा मनोमयः | तेनैष पूर्णः ||२.३.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmāt prāṇamayāt | anyo'ntara ātmā manomayaḥ | tenaiṣa pūrṇaḥ ||2.3.1||
verses.english_translation: Different from this one made of prana is another inner Self made of mind. By that, this one is filled.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणमयात् अन्यः — प्राणकोशात् सूक्ष्मतरः — मनोमयः — मनःकोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Within the prana-body is the mind-body (manomaya kosha). The mind-body is subtler and fills the prana-body. The mind includes the five sense organs and the mind (manas). This is the third sheath.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्मान्मनोमयात् | अन्योऽन्तर आत्मा विज्ञानमयः | तेनैष पूर्णः ||२.४.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmān manomayāt | anyo'ntara ātmā vijñānamayaḥ | tenaiṣa pūrṇaḥ ||2.4.1||
verses.english_translation: Different from this one made of mind is another inner Self made of intellect. By that, this one is filled.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयात् अन्यः — मनःकोशात् सूक्ष्मतरः — विज्ञानमयः — बुद्धिकोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Within the mind-body is the intellect-body (vijnanamaya kosha). The intellect (buddhi) is the faculty of discrimination and decision. It is subtler than the mind and is the fourth sheath.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्माद्विज्ञानमयात् | अन्योऽन्तर आत्माऽऽनन्दमयः | तेनैष पूर्णः ||२.५.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmād vijñānamayāt | anyo'ntara ātmānandamayaḥ | tenaiṣa pūrṇaḥ ||2.5.1||
verses.english_translation: Different from this one made of intellect is another inner Self made of bliss. By that, this one is filled.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: विज्ञानमयात् अन्यः — बुद्धिकोशात् सूक्ष्मतरः — आनन्दमयः — आनन्दकोशः — कारणशरीरम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The innermost sheath is the bliss-body (anandamaya kosha), which corresponds to deep sleep. It is the causal body. Beyond even this is the true Self — pure consciousness — which is not a sheath at all but the witness of all five sheaths.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं न निन्द्यात् | तद्व्रतम् | प्राणो वा अन्नम् | शरीरमन्नादम् | प्राणे शरीरं प्रतिष्ठितम् | शरीरे प्राणः प्रतिष्ठितः ||३.७.१||
verses.transliteration: annaṃ na nindyāt | tad vratam | prāṇo vā annam | śarīram annādam | prāṇe śarīraṃ pratiṣṭhitam | śarīre prāṇaḥ pratiṣṭhitaḥ ||3.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: the body is the eater of food
the body is established in prana

verses.english_translation: One should not despise food — that is the vow. Prana is indeed food; the body is the eater of food. The body is established in prana; prana is established in the body.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नम् न निन्द्यात् — अन्नम् ब्रह्मरूपम् — तस्य निन्दा न कार्या। प्राणः अन्नम् — प्राणः अन्नेन जीवति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Food should not be despised because food is Brahman in manifest form. The entire teaching of the Taittiriya shows that Brahman manifests as food, prana, mind, intellect, and bliss. To despise food is to despise Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: यदो ह्येवैष एतस्मिन्नदृश्येऽनात्म्येऽनिरुक्तेऽनिलयनेऽभयं प्रतिष्ठां विन्दते | अथ सोऽभयं गतो भवति ||२.७.१||
verses.transliteration: yado hy evaiṣa etasminn adṛśye'nātmye'nirukte'nilayane'bhayaṃ pratiṣṭhāṃ vindate | atha so'bhayaṃ gato bhavati ||2.7.1||
verses.english_translation: When one finds fearless support in that which is unseen, bodiless, unutterable, and supportless — then one has gone to fearlessness.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अदृश्ये — इन्द्रियागोचरे। अनात्म्ये — शरीररहिते। अनिरुक्ते — वाचामगोचरे। अभयम् प्रतिष्ठाम् विन्दते — ब्रह्मणि आश्रयम् लभते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: When the seeker finds support in Brahman — which is beyond the senses, bodiless, beyond speech, and self-supporting — then all fear vanishes. Fear arises from duality; in the non-dual Brahman, there is no 'other' to fear.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै भूमा तत्सुखम् | नाल्पे सुखमस्ति | भूमैव सुखम् | भूमा त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्यः ||२.८.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai bhūmā tat sukham | nālpe sukham asti | bhūmaiva sukham | bhūmā tv eva vijijñāsitavyaḥ ||2.8.1||

word_meanings.meaning: there is no bliss in the finite

verses.english_translation: The infinite is bliss. There is no bliss in the finite. The infinite alone is bliss. The infinite alone should be inquired into.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: भूमा — अनन्तम् — तत् सुखम्। अल्पे — परिच्छिन्ने — सुखम् नास्ति। भूमा — ब्रह्म — एव सुखम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The infinite (Brahman) is bliss. Finite things cannot give true bliss — only temporary pleasure. The infinite Brahman alone is the source of all bliss. Therefore Brahman should be inquired into — this is the purpose of the Upanishad.

verses.sanskrit: यदा ह्येवैष एतस्मिन्नुदरमन्तरं कुरुते | अथ तस्य भयं भवति ||२.७.१||
verses.transliteration: yadā hy evaiṣa etasminn udaram antaraṃ kurute | atha tasya bhayaṃ bhavati ||2.7.1||
verses.english_translation: When one makes even a slight separation in this (Brahman), then fear arises for him.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतस्मिन् — ब्रह्मणि — उदरम् अन्तरम् कुरुते — भेदम् पश्यति — तस्य भयम् भवति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: When one sees even a slight separation or difference in Brahman — when one perceives duality — fear arises. Fear is the product of duality. In the non-dual Brahman, there is no 'other' to fear. This is why the Upanishad teaches unity.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | अन्नाद्धि खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते | अन्नेन जातानि जीवन्ति | अन्नं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.२.१||
verses.transliteration: annaṃ brahmeti vyajānāt | annād dhi khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | annena jātāni jīvanti | annaṃ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.2.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: food is Brahman. For from food alone are all these beings born; by food they live once born; into food they return at death.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नम् ब्रह्म — अन्नम् ब्रह्मरूपम्। अन्नात् जायन्ते — अन्नरसात् शरीरम् उत्पद्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bhrigu's first realization is that food is Brahman. All beings are born from food, live by food, and return to food. But this is only the first step — Pippalada sends him back to inquire further, leading to deeper realizations.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणो ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | प्राणाद्धि खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते | प्राणेन जातानि जीवन्ति | प्राणं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.३.१||
verses.transliteration: prāṇo brahmeti vyajānāt | prāṇād dhi khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | prāṇena jātāni jīvanti | prāṇaṃ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.3.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: prana is Brahman. For from prana alone are all these beings born; by prana they live; into prana they return.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः ब्रह्म — प्राणः ब्रह्मरूपः। प्राणात् जायन्ते — प्राणशक्त्या शरीरम् उत्पद्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bhrigu's second realization is that prana is Brahman. Prana is subtler than food — it is the life-force that animates the body. But Varuna sends him back again for deeper inquiry.

verses.sanskrit: मनो ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | मनसो ह्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते | मनसा जातानि जीवन्ति | मनः प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.४.१||
verses.transliteration: mano brahmeti vyajānāt | manaso hy eva khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | manasā jātāni jīvanti | manaḥ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.4.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: mind is Brahman. For from mind alone are all these beings born; by mind they live; into mind they return.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनः ब्रह्म — मनः ब्रह्मरूपम्। मनसः जायन्ते — मनःसंकल्पात् सृष्टिः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bhrigu's third realization is that mind is Brahman. Mind is subtler than prana — it is the faculty of thought and will. But Varuna sends him back once more for the deepest realization.

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञानं ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | विज्ञानाद्ध्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते | विज्ञानेन जातानि जीवन्ति | विज्ञानं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.५.१||
verses.transliteration: vijñānaṃ brahmeti vyajānāt | vijñānād dhy eva khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | vijñānena jātāni jīvanti | vijñānaṃ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.5.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: consciousness is Brahman. For from consciousness alone are all these beings born; by consciousness they live; into consciousness they return.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: विज्ञानम् ब्रह्म — बुद्धिः ब्रह्मरूपा। विज्ञानात् जायन्ते — चैतन्यशक्त्या सृष्टिः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bhrigu's fourth realization is that consciousness (vijnana/intellect) is Brahman. This is deeper than mind. But Varuna sends him back one final time for the ultimate realization — that bliss (ananda) is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: मातृदेवो भव | पितृदेवो भव | आचार्यदेवो भव | अतिथिदेवो भव ||१.११.२||
verses.transliteration: mātṛdevo bhava | pitṛdevo bhava | ācāryadevo bhava | atithidevo bhava ||1.11.2||

word_meanings.meaning: let your mother be your god
let your father be your god
let your teacher be your god
let the guest be your god

verses.english_translation: Let your mother be your god. Let your father be your god. Let your teacher be your god. Let the guest be your god.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मातृदेवः — माता देवः यस्य — सः। पितृदेवः — पिता देवः यस्य। आचार्यदेवः — आचार्यः देवः यस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The teacher's farewell instruction includes these four great ethical teachings. Mother, father, teacher, and guest are to be treated as gods — with reverence and service. This is the foundation of dharmic life in the Vedic tradition.

verses.sanskrit: यान्यनवद्यानि कर्माणि | तानि सेवितव्यानि | नो इतराणि | यान्यस्माकं सुचरितानि | तानि त्वयोपास्यानि ||१.११.३||
verses.transliteration: yāny anavadyāni karmāṇi | tāni sevitavyāni | no itarāṇi | yāny asmākaṃ sucaritāni | tāni tvayopāsyāni ||1.11.3||
verses.english_translation: Blameless actions should be practiced, not others. Whatever good conduct of ours you have seen — that should be followed by you.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अनवद्यानि — निर्दोषाणि — कर्माणि — सेवितव्यानि। सुचरितानि — शोभनाचरणानि — उपास्यानि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The teacher instructs the student to practice only blameless actions and to follow the good conduct of the teacher. This is the practical ethical teaching of the Taittiriya — dharmic living is the foundation for spiritual knowledge.
